mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that systematically depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that overrides rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4 percent, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that immediately suppresses S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector that drags down equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative HOUST shocks are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.
